paper_title: LORIS: A Lightweight Free-Climbing Robot for Extreme Terrain Exploration
source_file: 机理文献/条件备选/05_ LORIS A Lightweight Free-Climbing Robot for Extreme Terrain Exploration.pdf

render_review.toolchain: Poppler pdfinfo/pdftoppm executables from the bundled runtime; pdfplumber for per-page text extraction.
render_review.all_pages: All seven pages were rendered at 150 dpi and visually checked in a contact sheet.
render_review.notes: The bundled wrapper scripts pointed to a missing bin path, so the underlying Poppler executables were called directly. Poppler reported missing display-font mappings for Symbol and ArialUnicode, but visual review confirmed that all retained Greek symbols, superscripts, subscripts, axes, legends, units and numerical labels rendered legibly.

sections_checked: References checked for document completeness and cited-origin boundaries only

equations_checked.equation: Eqs. (1)-(2), carriage tangential force and net x-z force
equations_checked.notes: The cosine projection, leading minus sign in the external-force sum, column-vector order [cos(alpha_i), sin(alpha_i)], beta bounds and cos(beta-alpha_i)<0 disengagement condition were checked on the rendered page. Retained as E1.

equations_checked.equation: Eqs. (3)-(10), passive-wrist force/moment balance and disengagement constraints
equations_checked.notes: All x/y/z subscripts, signs, w/2 term, pivot coordinates, pull-off inequalities and nonnegative base-contact force were checked against the page image. The paper cites phi_slip from prior work and does not provide a numerical value. Retained as E2.

Objective, 3D force and moment equilibrium, tangential bounds, binormal angle bounds, pull-off-margin constraint and nonnegative tail force were checked. The meaning and sign of c were verified from the adjacent prose. Retained as E3.

equations_checked.equation: Unnumbered force estimate and Eq. (18), grasp-map null-space force control
equations_checked.notes: The mappings f_hat_i=J_i^{-T} tau_hat_i and A=I-G^T pinv(G^T), the leading negative sign, k_p placement and GA delta X=0 property were checked visually. Retained as E4.

tables_checked.notes: LORIS terrain class, 3 DoF per limb, 3.2 kg mass, 0.20 m/min speed and 1 g entry were checked. Only the LORIS row is used as a scale reference.

All five rows, DIG state, payload, total mass, failures/steps and speed were checked. The 9/141, 4/177, 6/94 and 3/86 ratios agree with the printed percentages. The evidence card retains only the comparisons needed for the mechanism claims.

figures_checked.reason: Outdoor robot appearance is not needed once the tufa field-test boundary is recorded in text.
Defines the carriage geometry, force/displacement angles, load-direction bounds, carriage-count tradeoff and embedded spine flexure in one non-redundant figure.
Defines the three contacts, pivot coordinates and normalized pivot-location map used by the passive-wrist equations.
The robot hardware overview is adequately represented by the retained topology and parameter table; it does not add a separate mechanism result.
Directly compares optimized force directions and the 1.88/1.89/2.65/5.53 N margins for axial versus splayed and vertical versus inward grippers.
Terrain photographs are not quantitative morphology evidence; the relevant conditions and outcomes are retained from Table II and the text.

omitted_sections: General planetary-science motivation and broad prior-robot comparison
Ordinary manufacturing, actuator, battery, computer and camera details not affecting the retained mechanics
Teleoperation and future path-planning discussion beyond the stated experimental limitation

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.2, Section II.A and Fig. 2c
uncertain_items.detail: Each carriage is a lumped linear spring and any reverse carriage displacement is treated as grasp failure. The analysis does not identify how 13 spines within a carriage share load or survive partial disengagement.
phi_slip is attributed to prior work and is not numerically specified for the LORIS gripper. The pivot map is therefore verified as a model result but not reproducible from this paper alone.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.4-5, Section III.A, Eqs. (14)-(16) and Fig. 5
uncertain_items.detail: The paper does not report the complete numerical values or calibration procedures for f_min, f_max, theta_max and phi_max. The printed margins are retained as scenario-specific outputs, not universal capacities.
Motor-current torque estimates are moving-average filtered, but the paper gives no independent force-sensor calibration, error distribution, delay or saturation study.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.5-6, Section IV and Table II
uncertain_items.detail: Failure rate is falls divided by counted travel steps; recoverable slips and recovery steps are excluded. A teleoperator selected footholds and freed the robot when necessary, and different irregular-surface rows use different hardware, payload and DIG conditions.

known_warnings: The two-carriage advantage is conditional on the equal-stiffness directional-spring model and must not be generalized to fewer microspines being universally better.
The passive-wrist model assumes a frictionless base contact and at least three contacts; highly convex footholds can violate this contact set.
Passive yaw alignment requires a significant in-plane gravity component and is explicitly not generalized to fully inverted or microgravity climbing.
Figure 5 adhesion margins are linear-program outputs under undisclosed full parameter calibration, not measured gripper capacities.
The null-space controller assumes accurate grasp and limb mappings and does not include actuator saturation, contact switching or state-dependent compliance.
Only the two cinderblock rows provide a near-controlled DIG comparison; basalt, slag and tufa rows must not be compared as an isolated DIG experiment.
The cinderblock sample is small and assisted, and no statistical significance test is reported.
A single gripper loss is often irrecoverable in the three-support phase; the paper does not model dynamic redistribution or fall recovery.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs in evidence_card.md; within the explicitly stated 3500-character extension for four independent evidence chains.
